body_txt: This is often a case of Writers Cannot Do Math and miscalculating the hypothetical age of a character's grandparents, despite their parents generally being a much more realistic age. Furthermore, centenarians (or late 90s) are a common age for fictional grandparents due to the need for a "100th birthday milestone" plot. Of course, this is Truth in Television to an extent. While the average age of parents when their kids are born is in the 20s or 30s age range, Teen Pregnancies do occur, which often results in more generations in the family much longer than typical. On the opposite end of the spectrum, menopause takes time and occurs at different ages depending on the woman while fathers do not have an age limit on when they can impregnate a woman, which results in parents having children at a much older age. See Absurdly Elderly Mother and Absurdly Elderly Father for when the parents are old enough to be their child's grandparents and Practically Different Generations for when an older sibling (usually half or step) is old enough to be their younger sibling's parent. May involve Older Nephew, Younger Uncle or Senior Citizen's Even Older Parent . Compare and contrast Hollywood Old . May overlap with Long-Lived , and in some cases may be justified if the grandparent(s) are of a species that has a long lifespan. Examples of grandparents who are a realistic age but look much older do not qualify for this trope.
